commit.message:
fix: remove broken startOffset optimization for build discovery

The startOffset calculation assumed Prow build IDs increased by
~80B/day, but they actually increase by ~1.1 quadrillion per
build (~48h interval). This caused jobs with longer intervals
to only discover 3 builds instead of 8.

Simplified to always list all builds via GCS JSON API (single
request for <1000 builds, which covers all CAPZ jobs).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix bugs and improve UX across 8 areas

- jobPhase(): remove redundant '|| j.travelStart' in 'arrived' guard
  (condition 2 already catches travelStart+no-travelEnd, making the OR
  branch dead code and potentially misleading to future readers)

- Active job indicator: highlight the active ticket card in the list
  with an accent border and '● Active' badge so techs can spot it at a
  glance; renderTicketList() now applies .ticket-active when
  S.activeJob?.ticketId matches

- Photo size limit: reject files > 10 MB in addPhoto() before
  readAsDataURL() to prevent tab crashes from large images

- Autofill toast: distinguish 'both fields filled' vs 'start only'
  vs 'nothing to fill' instead of always saying 'Time fields populated'

- submitFinishLog: use Math.max(1, onMins) instead of onMins || 1 to
  make the 1-minute floor explicit and intentional

- Finish sheet: show ticket summary row so techs can confirm they are
  logging against the right job; also escape ticketId properly with esc()

- Refresh detail view after job completion: call renderDetailBody() and
  renderTicketList() after a successful finish so job-action buttons,
  activity log, and photo grid are not left in a stale state

- beforeunload warning: prompt the browser's leave-page dialog whenever
  S.photos.length > 0 or S.activeJob is set, preventing accidental
  loss of in-memory photos or active job state on reload

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: agenda system, bug fix (#123)

* feat: topic agenda system with concept-based onboarding

Replace flat skill selection with a structured topic agenda that
supports role-based concept browsing, prerequisite-aware ordering,
and configurable assessment thoroughness (quick/standard/thorough).

Backend:
- Add AgendaItem, TopicStatus, Thoroughness state types
- Add build_agenda pipeline node between calibration and assessment
- Add GET /roles/{role_id}/concepts endpoint with topo-sorted concepts
- Add display_name field to all knowledge base YAML concepts
- Infer confidence for un-assessed concepts from assessed prerequisites
- Dynamic question budget based on agenda size and thoroughness
- Conclude assessment when no pending topics remain

Frontend:
- Replace SkillBrowser with ConceptBrowser grouped by level
- Fetch concepts per role/level via new API endpoint
- Add max 10 concept selection limit with disabled state
- Show estimated question count from thoroughness setting

* fix: auto-create openlearning_test DB on postgres container init

Mount an init script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ so the test
database is created alongside the main database on first container
startup. Prevents local test failures after volume recreation.

   skill_ids produce no matches, keeping agenda consistent with

Includes protocol-only tests (no credentials), provider-specific contract tests (metadata, files, diffs, content, negative cases), and shared test infrastructure for process management and response parsing. Adjusted file path assertion to ignore leading slashes in AdoFileDiffContractTests for robustness. Updated expected status of "src/Calculator.cs" from "edit" to "modified" in AdoTestExpectations to align with API terminology.

* Fixed contract tests

* Improve CLI tool hiding in smoke tests; better diagnostics

Enhance BuildRestrictedPathEnv to robustly hide gh/az: on Windows, filter PATH more strictly; on Linux/macOS, prepend shadow scripts to PATH. Update XML docs to clarify platform-specific logic. In FullInstallSmokeTests, include stdout/stderr in failure and timeout messages for improved troubleshooting.

* Optimize smoke test process startup and error handling

Use compile-time build config and --no-build for dotnet run in test helpers to avoid redundant builds. Add robust error handling for stdin/stdout pipe races and process exit timing, improving test speed and reliability.

* Improve robustness of FullInstallSmokeTests process handling

- Use compile-time build config and --no-build for dotnet pack to avoid MSBuild/VBCSCompiler pipe handle issues in parallel test runs
- Add 60s timeout to init command to prevent hangs
- Wrap all process stdin writes/closes in try/catch (IOException) to handle early process exits
- Handle OperationCanceledException on pipe reads; force-kill process and return empty output if pipes are held open by child processes
- Include timeout duration in error messages for better diagnostics
- Align process handling with patterns used in CliProcessHelper for consistency and reliability

* Refactor process helpers to avoid WaitForExitAsync hangs

Replace WaitForExitAsync with WaitForExit(timeout) in smoke test helpers to prevent hangs when child processes inherit pipe handles. Pipe reads now use a separate CancellationTokenSource and a post-exit grace period to ensure output is drained without blocking indefinitely. Improves reliability of test infrastructure when running dotnet, az, or gh shims.

* Harden full install smoke test against CI CLI interference

Restrict PATH during init to shadow az/gh CLI tools with dummy scripts, preventing hangs from interactive az login on CI runners. RunProcessAsync now accepts environment overrides to support this. Improves test reliability in environments with pre-installed CLIs.

* Exclude ContractAdo and ContractGitHub tests in CI/CD

Updated ci.yml, publish.yml, and release.yml to filter out tests with Category=ContractAdo and Category=ContractGitHub during dotnet test runs. This ensures these contract tests are not executed as part of the standard CI, publish, or release workflows.

* Hide create-pr.md prompt for the end user, because it's not ready for deploy

The create-pr.md Copilot prompt is no longer copied to .github/prompts/ by the rebuss-pure init command; it remains in embedded resources for future use. Documentation and tests are updated to reflect that only review-pr.md and self-review.md are deployed. The #create-pr command and prompt are not yet available to users.

Change config resolution to prefer detected repo info over cached.
Update docs and tests to match new precedence (user > detected > cached).
Add comprehensive tests for GitHubConfigurationResolver, including

* Always overwrite prompts/instructions on init; add instructions dir

Init now always copies prompt and instruction files from embedded resources to .github/prompts/ and .github/instructions/, overwriting any existing files to ensure updates are deployed. Adds .github/instructions/ for Copilot custom instructions. Updates tests and docs to reflect new behavior and directory. File copying occurs before authentication, so files are created even if auth fails. Contributors should only edit embedded source files.

* Improve GitHub PR parsing, CLI token expiry, and config routing

- Distinguish merged vs. non-merged closed PRs ("completed" vs. "abandoned") in PR parser and tests
- Add ParseWithCommits for efficient PR metadata and SHA extraction
- Optimize diff provider: single-parse PR metadata, parallel file content fetch
- GitHubApiClient: only return null for 404, throw for other errors
- Auth handler: avoid retry on rate-limit 403 (X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0)
- Reduce GitHub CLI token cache lifetime from 365 days to 24 hours
- Route PATs and repo config to correct provider section based on CLI args; normalize provider names
- Update comments for clarity and fix encoding issues

* Add CancellationToken support to GitHub API client

All IGitHubApiClient methods now accept an optional CancellationToken, and the implementation passes it to all HttpClient calls. Internal helpers and all consumers (diff, file content, metadata providers) are updated to propagate cancellation. GitHubCliTokenProvider now uses a linked timeout token for process output reading. This enables prompt cancellation of network and process operations, improving responsiveness and reliability.

commit.message:
fix(uniflight): eliminate race condition in panic propagation tests (#342)

Replace tokio::spawn + sleep-based synchronization with eager cell
registration via sequential execute() calls and tokio::join!.

The original tests relied on sleep(10ms) to ensure the follower task was
polled before the leader completed. In tokio's single-threaded runtime,
the leader could finish and clean up the DashMap entry before the
follower task was ever polled, causing the follower to become a new
leader and return Ok instead of Err(LeaderPanicked).

The fix exploits the fact that execute() eagerly calls
get_or_create_cell() before returning the future. Creating both futures
sequentially guarantees the follower joins the leader's cell
deterministically, with no timing dependency.

Co-authored-by: Kateřina Churanová <katerina.churanova@microsoft.com>

commit.message:
feat: DevTunnel, snapfeed feedback, bandwidth stats, Gastown, Go TUI

## DevTunnel support
- Named persistent tunnels (stable URL across restarts)
- Auto-detection: prefers devtunnel, falls back to cloudflare
- CLI: --tunnel-provider auto|devtunnel|cloudflare

## Snapfeed UI feedback (@microsoft/snapfeed)
- GitHub Device Flow auth (user's own identity)
- Server-side token storage (~/.remux/github-token)
- GitHub OAuth proxy endpoints (CORS bypass)
- Telemetry endpoint: POST /api/telemetry/events (SQLite)
- Issues include: message, context, console errors, network log,
  session replay, screenshot (opt-in)

## Server extensions
- Terminal state tracker (@xterm/headless, mosh-like diffs)

commit.message:
feat(seatbelt): introduce chaos injection middleware (#335)

## Add chaos injection middleware

Introduces `Injection`, a new middleware under
`seatbelt::chaos::injection` that replaces service output with a
user-provided value at a configurable probability. Gated behind the
`chaos-injection` feature flag.

### API

```rust
use seatbelt::chaos::injection::Injection;

// Callback-based: receives consumed input
Injection::layer("my_injection", &context)
    .rate(0.1)
    .output_with(|input, _args| format!("fault for {input}"));

// Fixed value (cloned each time)
Injection::layer("my_injection", &context)
    .rate(0.1)
    .output("injected error".to_string());
```

### Highlights

- **Type-state builder** enforces `rate` + `output` are set before use
- **Dual `Service` impls** — `layered::Service` and
`tower_service::Service`
- **`rate()` clamps** to `[0.0, 1.0]` instead of panicking
- **`output_with()` receives consumed input** for context-aware fault
generation
- **`InjectionConfig`** with serde support for file-based configuration
- **Telemetry** — `resilience.event` counter (`chaos.injection`) +
structured log
- **`enable_if` / `disable`** for conditional activation

### Changes

- New feature: `chaos-injection` (depends on `fastrand`)
- New module: `src/chaos/injection/` (8 files)
- New example: `examples/chaos_injection.rs`
- New integration tests: `tests/chaos_injection.rs` (18 tests via
rstest, layered + tower)
- Updated `lib.rs`, `context.rs`, `metrics.rs` feature gates

---------

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)
![Modal closed with success
toast](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f085375-e2f1-4f06-bef8-ce18b86dd2e1)

fix: resolve session.run() query param collision in cross-video and entity search

Root cause: Neo4j session.run(query, **params) conflicts when params dict
contains a 'query' key — Python sees duplicate values for the first positional
parameter. This caused 'Session.run() got multiple values for argument query'
on every agent tool call, producing 0% accuracy in evaluation.

Changes:
- Rename Cypher param \ → \ in all 6 fulltext query constants
- Use parameters= dict instead of **kwargs spread in _run_query and callers
- Add sanitize_fulltext_query() to cross_video_search_service and knowledge_graph
- Fix search_entities() and search_frames_by_description() same collision bug

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: harden search engine — Lucene escaping, bounded graph expansion, structured agent errors

Root cause of 0% agent accuracy in Video-MME evaluation: queries containing
Lucene special chars (parentheses, slashes) crashed fulltext search, causing
the agent to return generic error on every question.

Changes:
- Add sanitize_fulltext_query() with compiled regex for all 16 Lucene operators
- Refactor _fulltext_search() into single DRY method with conditional WHERE
- Replace double-traversal graph expansion (subgraphNodes + unbounded
  shortestPath) with single apoc.path.expandConfig YIELD path pattern
- Return structured error field in agent response for better diagnostics
- Add 27 unit tests covering escaping edge cases and integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
redesign: anti-AI aesthetic — warm editorial logbook UI

Complete frontend rewrite with intentionally non-AI design choices:
- Light-first (warm cream #f8f5f0), not dark-first
- Serif display font (Fraunces) + humanist sans (Karla), not Inter/Outfit
- Zero rounded corners — sharp edges throughout
- Warm earthy palette (brick red, stone, olive), not blue/purple
- Dense table-row layout, not spacious card grid
- Paper grain texture overlay
- Product tabs instead of colored pills
- Dashed rule separators, not drop shadows
- Single accent color (vermillion), not rainbow product colors
- Print-friendly, information-dense reference aesthetic

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
